summary: Tested 3 new features: (1) ConsequenceNumberBlock pricing policy, (2) REACH widget email unification with Ground 0 bottom form, (3) Direct bottom email form submission. All 3 features working correctly.

minor.issue: Returns 422 Unprocessable Entity when email uses non-standard TLD like .test (e.g. test@launchpath.test). Pydantic EmailStr validation rejects these. Real email addresses (e.g. @example.com) work fine. The REACH widget silently handles this in catch block so UX still shows confirmation - but the data is not saved to MailerLite when such emails are used.

critical_code_review_comments: The REACH widget email submit (handleEmailSubmit) catches errors and still calls onEmailCaptured() + sets submitted=true even when the API returns 422/500. This means the G0 bottom form shows confirmation even if data was NOT saved. This is intentional by design (UX-first) but worth noting.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ground 0 page route is /ground-0-briefing (not /ground0). All 3 features tested and passing: (1) $2,500 removed from ConsequenceNumberBlock, $10,000-$25,000+ + The LaunchPath Standard + 'Costs less than one audit failure.' visible. (2) REACH widget email captured auto-submits bottom G0 form and shows 'Sent to: [email]' in confirmation. (3) Direct bottom form submit also works and shows confirmation with email address.
